sections.title: 1. Privacy Rule: Patient Rights & Data Governance

items.text: Is the Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP) current, prominently displayed, and provided to all patients at the first point of service?
Is there a documented process to provide patients access to their PHI within 30 days of a request?
Are Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) on file and reviewed annually for all third-party vendors with PHI access?
Is the 'Minimum Necessary' standard applied to all internal uses and external disclosures of PHI?
Are valid, written authorizations obtained and filed for all non-routine uses of PHI?
Is an 'Accounting of Disclosures' log maintained for non-routine sharing (e.g., law enforcement or judicial requests)?
Is there an operational workflow for handling and documenting patient requests to amend their health records?
Can the system honor patient requests to restrict PHI disclosure to health plans for services paid for entirely out-of-pocket?
Are de-identification procedures (Safe Harbor or Expert Determination) verified before PHI is used for research or analytics?
Has a comprehensive, system-wide Security Risk Analysis (SRA) been conducted or updated within the last 12 months?
Is a formal Risk Management Plan active, with specific timelines for remediating vulnerabilities identified in the SRA?
Are security awareness training records (including attendance and curriculum) maintained for all workforce members?
Is there a documented and tested Contingency Plan addressing data backup, disaster recovery, and emergency mode operations?
Is a formal 'Evaluation' (§ 164.308(a)(8)) performed after major environmental or operational changes to verify continued compliance?
Is a sanction policy documented and consistently applied for workforce members who violate security policies?
Is physical access to facilities containing ePHI (server rooms, data centers) restricted to authorized personnel and recorded in an access log?
Are workstations that access ePHI physically positioned or shielded to prevent unauthorized viewing by the public or other staff?
Does the hardware inventory track the physical location, movement, and current owner of all portable devices containing ePHI?
Is there a documented process for the secure 'purging' or physical destruction of electronic media containing ePHI before disposal or reuse?
Are maintenance records kept for physical security components like locks, cameras, and badge readers?
Is unique user identification enforced for all system access, and is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) required for remote access?
Are audit logs generated, timestamped, and reviewed regularly for all systems and applications processing ePHI?
Are technical mechanisms (e.g., hashing or checksums) used to ensure ePHI has not been altered or destroyed in an unauthorized manner?
Is encryption enforced for ePHI both at rest (on servers and mobile devices) and in transit (via TLS 1.2+ or equivalent)?
Are systems configured with automatic session locks or logoffs after a defined period of user inactivity?
Is a formal '4-factor' risk assessment performed and documented for every security incident to determine if it constitutes a reportable breach?
Are breach notification templates on file and verified to contain the five legally required elements per § 164.404(c)?
Is there a defined workflow for notifying prominent media outlets if a breach affects more than 500 residents of a single jurisdiction?
Are all individual notifications sent without unreasonable delay and within the 60-day legal window?
Is there a centralized incident log tracking all potential compromises, including those determined not to be breaches?
